includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Contamination Removal Database

contaminant.name: Degraded Polymer Deposits
contaminant.description: Plastic-residue contamination, it manifests as melted, degraded, or oxidized layers from heat exposure or UV degradation, which distinguishes itself from inorganic rusts by its viscoelastic, non-brittle nature that clings irregularly. On metals such as steel and aluminum, the residue adheres tenaciously, influenced from surface porosity, while on glass it spreads thinly yet persistently, leading to challenges in removal due to its thermal sensitivity and potential for re-melting during cleaning. Laser cleaning proves effective, as the process vaporizes the residue selectively, that demonstrates preservation of the substrate integrity without mechanical abrasion.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Carbon Monoxide, Hydrogen Cyanide, Benzene - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation. WARNING: Benzene, Formaldehyde - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
